greeting: Hello! I can help with billing questions. Which charge on your statement looks incorrect?
fallback: If you don't recognize a charge, let me look it up. Do you have the date and amount?

rules.response: I understand this charge does not look familiar. Let me look it up. Your account shows a Basic plan at $19.99/mo. The $49.99 Premium Plan Upgrade charge is unusual. I will flag this to our billing team for investigation. Would you like to also dispute this charge?
I can see the $49.99 Premium Plan Upgrade charge on May 15th. This does not match your current Basic plan. I will escalate this as an unauthorized change to your account.

I can request a refund for this charge. Since it was unauthorized and from May 15th (within 30 days), it should qualify. I will initiate the refund process. You should see the credit within 5-7 business days.

I will treat this as a potentially unauthorized transaction. Let me document the charge and escalate to our fraud team. In the meantime, monitor your account for other suspicious activity and change your account password.
You are correct. Your account is on the Basic tier at $19.99/mo. The $49.99 Premium Plan Upgrade was applied on May 15th without a plan change request. This appears to be an error on our end.

I understand you may want to speak with someone directly. I have noted your request and escalated this to our billing department. A specialist will reach out within 24 hours. Your case reference is BILL-2026-0515.
You are welcome. I have initiated the refund for the unauthorized $49.99 Premium Plan Upgrade charge. You will receive a confirmation email shortly. Is there anything else I can help with?
